The state sanctioned persecution of Pakistan's Ahmadiyya Muslim Community presents a critical blockade to justice and humanity

* * *
# [As 1.6 Billion Muslims Celebrate Eid--One Muslim Community Faces Horrid Obstacles](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=145659974&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ1NjU5OTc0LCJpYXQiOjE3MTg1NTQ0MjcsImV4cCI6MTcyMTE0NjQyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zJ53gg9yY2U5WS3Gxbq2WQHjZpJICcHBLNLMeUBJMKw)
### The state sanctioned persecution of Pakistan's Ahmadiyya Muslim Community presents a critical blockade to justice and humanity
| | [Qasim Rashid](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jun 16
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=145659974&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ1NjU5OTc0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTcxODU1NDQyNywiZXhwIjoxNzIxMTQ2NDI3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lv5vLrijcrbara8QQFs079hfSVh1uPuDXRo60gwLnQM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..t3CiyNH9OQNsFCpaGtIOgG7eV-3qzrij75PvXO66Dp8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145659974)
Eid Mubarak!
Let's start with a quick thought exercise. Imagine if tomorrow the United States government passed a constitutional amendment declaring all Catholics as not Christian. Then imagine if the government added criminal provisions that any Catholic identifying or acting Christian would be fined, arrested, and given up to the death penalty. Additionally imagine that when a Catholic went to register to vote, they would be forced to choose between accepting that they were "non-Christian" or else sign a declaration denouncing the Pope and Catholicism. And finally, imagine if all books, websites, universities, even apps addressing Catholicism were banned, and Catholics using these tools to speak out about their persecution would face arrest or even death.
Hold that thought.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=145659974&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ1NjU5OTc0LCJpYXQiOjE3MTg1NTQ0MjcsImV4cCI6MTcyMTE0NjQyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zJ53gg9yY2U5WS3Gxbq2WQHjZpJICcHBLNLMeUBJMKw)
In a riveting speech days before Pakistan was officially born in 1947, Pakistan's Founding Father Muhammad Ali Jinnah addressed Pakistan's constituent assembly describing his vision for what the new nation would be. He declared:
> You are free; you are free to go to your temples, you are free to go to your mosques or to any other place of worship in this State of Pakistan. You may belong to any religion or caste or creed that has nothing to do with the business of the State.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b5103e64-b28f-4e0a-90f0-fa189e6f4acd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Muhammad Ali Jinnah (December 25, 1876 - September 11, 1948) Founder and First Governor General of Pakistan
But by 1974, a crack emerged in this foundation, and it has expanded ever since. And right now as some 1.6 billion Muslims celebrate our holiest day of the year, Eid ul Adha, commonly known as the Eid of Sacrifice, that chasm is ever painfully growing. To quickly share the significance of this Eid, I summarize from an article I wrote for [TIME Magazine nearly a decade ago](https://substack.com/redirect/4bef1962-9ae9-4947-b099-d57a75ad8c67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The Eid of Sacrifice is Islam's holiest holiday because it marks the end of the Hajj pilgrimage. It commemorates Prophet Abraham's willingness to sacrifice his son Ismael. God intervened and instead, he sacrificed a ram. On a deeper level, this Eid of Sacrifice asks Muslims to sacrifice our egos for the sake of service to God and service to humanity. The literal shift from sacrificing a person to sacrificing a ram set a precedent of the sanctity of all human life. This event established the principle that any act that takes innocent human life...is, in fact, repulsive to divine order.
But this article isn't about religious rites and rituals. It is about a principle that applies to all humanity--universal freedom of conscience--and the systemic denial of that freedom to members of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community in nations like Pakistan.
For Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan, even celebrating the holiest day of the year is an act of defiance and potential death sentence. The thought exercise I started this article with regarding Catholics is not a hypothetical for Pakistan's Ahmadi Muslims, but the ground reality. It's as if Pakistani clerics already enacted Pakistan's version of Project 2025, except they did it way back in 1974, and now we've seen what a half century of that oppression has produced. It isn't pretty, or sustainable.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..t3CiyNH9OQNsFCpaGtIOgG7eV-3qzrij75PvXO66Dp8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145659974)
Last week extremists [shot and killed two Ahmadi Muslims in broad daylight](https://substack.com/redirect/2546cabd-e299-4ad1-af13-be0d4b361f25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Additionally, extremist lawyers are demanding Ahmadi Muslim lawyers [denounce their own faith](https://substack.com/redirect/43b004c7-e13d-449a-ba88-aba097acc45b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to practice law or join the BAR in Pakistan. As an Ahmadi Muslim lawyer myself, this is particularly painful.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/44edd2ab-ed16-49e9-997e-1b89dfa271cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The two most recent Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan martyred for their faith are Ghulam Sarwar (left) and Rahat Ahmad Bajwah (right). Source: International Human Rights Committee.
In the past, I've written and published extensive [peer reviewed law review articles on the systemic persecution of Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan](https://substack.com/redirect/faf3fcbb-d97d-4df1-a1f0-033822701aac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but I write this to provide an easier to digest contemporary update to that ongoing persecution and violence--and why it absolutely impacts us here in the United States and globally outside of Pakistan.
As I wrote for [Just Security back in 2021](https://substack.com/redirect/e043d222-64cc-4866-ab0f-1ea109136f2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The alliance between the two nations has sent $70 billion in economic and military aid to Pakistan since Pakistan's founding. If not for the sake of sheer justice, then at least for the sake of protecting American interests, President Joe Biden must hold U.S. ally Pakistan accountable to repeal its [discriminatory anti-Ahmadi legislation](https://substack.com/redirect/bee6f81a-2826-45f1-b73b-c913cc2a7a2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and actions. While the legislation particularly targets Ahmadi Muslims, it tragically also enables societal discrimination and violence against Pakistan's Christian, Sikh, Hindu, and Shia communities.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ae6508e1-9af0-438c-a6d6-458eaad6df9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Debris remains from an Ahmadi mosque that was demolished by an angry mob May, 24, 2018, in Sialkot, Pakistan. Another Ahmadi place of worship was demolished by opponents in Lahore in September 2023. Credit: VOANews
And while Pakistan was founded as a secular nation that championed separation of religion and state, pseudo-intellectual clerics gained enough influence over the halls of power to enforce draconian and oppressive legislation targeting the minority Ahmadiyya Muslim Community. Today, Ahmadi Muslims exist in what can only be described as apartheid conditions. As the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom [reports](https://substack.com/redirect/bee6f81a-2826-45f1-b73b-c913cc2a7a2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> Ahmadis in Pakistan are subjected to severe legal restrictions and other forms of officially-sanctioned discrimination. Ahmadis are prevented by law from engaging in the full practice of their faith and may face criminal charges for a range of religious practices, including the use of religious terminology. In 1974, the constitution was amended to declare members of the Ahmadi religious community to be "non-Muslims." In 1984, basic acts of Ahmadi worship and interaction were made criminal offenses when sections B and C of Article 298 were added to the penal code. These amendments criminalized Ahmadis "posing" as Muslims, calling their places of worship "mosques," worshipping in non-Ahmadi mosques or public prayer rooms, performing the Muslim call to prayer, using the traditional Islamic greeting in public, or displaying the basic affirmations of the Muslim faith.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..t3CiyNH9OQNsFCpaGtIOgG7eV-3qzrij75PvXO66Dp8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145659974)
And as just one example of the absurdity of this persecution, [consider the case of an elderly Ahmadi Muslim lawyer](https://substack.com/redirect/66078829-f8d6-4260-bd57-b308a1802bb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> In April 2023, a 77-year-old lawyer from the Ahmadiyya Community was targeted within the premises of the Sindh High Court by some lawyers on account of his faith. He was accused of hurting the sentiments of Muslims by adding the religiously inclined word 'Syed' as a prefix to his name. However, critics argued that by using the prefix, which is only used by Muslims, the lawyer was trying to pose as a Muslim, and they did not accept that being of the Ahmadiyya faith, he was a Muslim.
You read that right. The blasphemy allegation was that his name, Syed, hurt the feelings of orthodox Muslims and therefore he is a criminal. The most prominent and violent attack on Ahmadi Muslims to date occurred on May 28, 2010, when Taliban extremists attacked two Ahmadi mosques in broad daylight, during Friday Jummah Prayer, and [killed 94 Ahmadis](https://substack.com/redirect/f4ec1894-0ebd-4fcb-b539-3adc8c0ccea5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/761073f1-782e-49b1-af54-9c802d46849d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
May 29, 2010, Ahmadi Muslims hold a mass funeral for 94 innocent worshippers mass murdered in Pakistan
Dozens to hundreds of Ahmadi Muslims are murdered annually with impunity, with hundreds to thousands more in prison, with hundreds _of_ thousands more denied the right to vote, run for office, practice their faith publicly, or even speak freely without the existential consequence of fine, arrest, or even the death penalty. And for those wondering how the authorities might discover that a person belongs to the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community? Ahmadis have special ID cards that forcibly identify their faith, making the targeted persecution an inevitability. The discriminatory laws on the books that ban Ahmadi Muslims from identifying as Muslim, practicing our faith, or even voting, remain on the books.
Meanwhile, and by all accounts and testimonies, Ahmadi Muslims are model citizens with a 99% literacy rate, build secular schools and hospitals globally providing free education and healthcare to millions, and have contributed to Pakistan world renowned juggernauts like Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner in [Dr. Abdus Salam](https://substack.com/redirect/89869667-140b-44d4-b426-32f284acf5cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0d0b1ec2-57ac-4ba3-b789-ab9ad4934440?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dr. Abdus Salam was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 for his contribution to the electroweak unification theory
But the Government of Pakistan has wiped even his memory from Pakistan's history books, [as they have wiped the word "Muslim" wiped off Salam's tombstone](https://substack.com/redirect/acfb0751-273f-4444-8513-cf584626a563?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8cadf694-f233-4621-955d-cb20ba90b692?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Nobel Laureate Dr. Abdus Salam's desecrated gravestone in Rabwah, Pakistan
Likewise, Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan was a Founding Father of Pakistan who worked closely with Jinnah. Khan was one of the most prolific lawyers and scholars of the 20th century, [and one of the most ardent champions for the Palestinian cause for liberation and self-determination](https://substack.com/redirect/837c8d74-84b1-4a91-b46f-196c12b1c1aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--yet in contemporary Pakistan he is all but forgotten on account of his devout faith as an Ahmadi Muslim.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/df149a11-6117-4cf7-be18-132dd31d971a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan (Right) served as President of the UN General Assembly
In a recent report to the United Nations, the Ahmadiyya Muslim Lawyers Association USA testified to the horrifying escalation that the Government of Pakistan is now engaging in by prosecuting Ahmadi Muslims on terrorism charges for the _' crime'_ of possessing and selling an [Ahmadi printed copy of the Qur'an](https://substack.com/redirect/a12502db-75de-417b-b172-1bae7c69c95a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> State authorities are also arresting and prosecuting Ahmadi Muslims as "terrorists" under the provisions of Pakistan's primary anti-terrorism legislation, the Anti-Terrorism Act of 1997, for possessing allegedly banned religious publications, arrested and sentenced to five years in prison on blasphemy and terrorism charges for selling copies of the Qur'an and other publications of the community.
**How ironic that while claiming to stand as the vanguard of Islam, Pakistan criminalizes possession of the Qur 'an itself in ways the West's most ardent Islamophobes could only dream of.** The result of this targeted persecution is that despite representing less than 1% of Pakistan's population, "Ahmadi Muslims account for almost 40% of all arrests under Pakistan's anti-blasphemy laws. Over 4,000 Ahmadi Muslims have faced criminal charges for simply practicing their Islamic faith."
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..t3CiyNH9OQNsFCpaGtIOgG7eV-3qzrij75PvXO66Dp8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145659974)
Perhaps most painful of all during this Eid of Sacrifice, is that while this Eid celebrates and marks the end of the Hajj Pilgrimage--a pilgrimage incumbent upon all able bodied Muslims--Pakistan bans Ahmadi Muslims from completing the Hajj. And still not content with a Hajj ban, [extremists in Pakistan have additionally banned Ahmadi Muslims from celebrating Eid itself](https://substack.com/redirect/1dd62757-c6ef-4f1f-b477-cf941fcb189e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Which brings me back to the vision Jinnah had for Pakistan. Indeed, Pakistan's first Constitution reflected the ideals of religious freedom and minority rights to date denied to Ahmadi Muslims. For example, the Preamble to Pakistan's Constitution declares that Pakistan shall be a state:
> Wherein adequate provision should be made for the minorities freely to profess and practise [sic] their religions and develop their culture. . .Wherein should be guaranteed fundamental rights including equality of status and of opportunity, equality before law, and freedom of thought, expression, belief, faith, worship and association, and social, economic, and political justice...; Wherein adequate provision shall be made to safeguard the legitimate interests of minorities...;
Accordingly, in an address to the United States less than a year after Pakistan's creation, Jinnah [declared and reaffirmed Pakistan's commitment](https://substack.com/redirect/49d4aa42-4b30-46c2-ab96-9893015c4506?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to freedom and pluralism to its new ally:
> In any case Pakistan is not going to be a theocratic State to be ruled by priests with a divine mission. We have many non-Muslims -- Hindus, Christians, and Parsis -- but they are all Pakistanis. They will enjoy the same rights and privileges as any other citizens and will play their rightful part in the affairs of Pakistan.
This Eid, as 1.6 billion Muslims celebrate, Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan will continue to seek that founding promise that has been denied to them for now half a century. While too many in Pakistani leadership and the religious cleric class may not believe in Jinnah's founding promise--Ahmadi Muslims do and always will, and will forever insist on playing our rightful part in the affairs of Pakistan.

clean_body: The state sanctioned persecution of Pakistan's Ahmadiyya Muslim Community presents a critical blockade to justice and humanity | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When the time is right, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $5/mo or $50/year subscription! The state sanctioned persecution of Pakistan's Ahmadiyya Muslim Community presents a critical blockade to justice and humanity | | | Jun 16 | | | | | | | | | | | | Eid Mubarak! Let's start with a quick thought exercise. Imagine if tomorrow the United States government passed a constitutional amendment declaring all Catholics as not Christian. Then imagine if the government added criminal provisions that any Catholic identifying or acting Christian would be fined, arrested, and given up to the death penalty. Additionally imagine that when a Catholic went to register to vote, they would be forced to choose between accepting that they were "non-Christian" or else sign a declaration denouncing the Pope and Catholicism. And finally, imagine if all books, websites, universities, even apps addressing Catholicism were banned, and Catholics using these tools to speak out about their persecution would face arrest or even death. Hold that thought. In a riveting speech days before Pakistan was officially born in 1947, Pakistan's Founding Father Muhammad Ali Jinnah addressed Pakistan's constituent assembly describing his vision for what the new nation would be. He declared: You are free; you are free to go to your temples, you are free to go to your mosques or to any other place of worship in this State of Pakistan. You may belong to any religion or caste or creed that has nothing to do with the business of the State. | | ---|---|--- Muhammad Ali Jinnah (December 25, 1876 - September 11, 1948) Founder and First Governor General of Pakistan But by 1974, a crack emerged in this foundation, and it has expanded ever since. And right now as some 1.6 billion Muslims celebrate our holiest day of the year, Eid ul Adha, commonly known as the Eid of Sacrifice, that chasm is ever painfully growing. To quickly share the significance of this Eid, I summarize from an article I wrote for : The Eid of Sacrifice is Islam's holiest holiday because it marks the end of the Hajj pilgrimage. It commemorates Prophet Abraham's willingness to sacrifice his son Ismael. God intervened and instead, he sacrificed a ram. On a deeper level, this Eid of Sacrifice asks Muslims to sacrifice our egos for the sake of service to God and service to humanity. The literal shift from sacrificing a person to sacrificing a ram set a precedent of the sanctity of all human life. This event established the principle that any act that takes innocent human life...is, in fact, repulsive to divine order. But this article isn't about religious rites and rituals. It is about a principle that applies to all humanity--universal freedom of conscience--and the systemic denial of that freedom to members of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community in nations like Pakistan. For Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan, even celebrating the holiest day of the year is an act of defiance and potential death sentence. The thought exercise I started this article with regarding Catholics is not a hypothetical for Pakistan's Ahmadi Muslims, but the ground reality. It's as if Pakistani clerics already enacted Pakistan's version of Project 2025, except they did it way back in 1974, and now we've seen what a half century of that oppression has produced. It isn't pretty, or sustainable. Last week extremists . Additionally, extremist lawyers are demanding Ahmadi Muslim lawyers to practice law or join the BAR in Pakistan. As an Ahmadi Muslim lawyer myself, this is particularly painful. | | ---|---|--- The two most recent Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan martyred for their faith are Ghulam Sarwar (left) and Rahat Ahmad Bajwah (right). Source: International Human Rights Committee. In the past, I've written and published extensive , but I write this to provide an easier to digest contemporary update to that ongoing persecution and violence--and why it absolutely impacts us here in the United States and globally outside of Pakistan. As I wrote for : The alliance between the two nations has sent $70 billion in economic and military aid to Pakistan since Pakistan's founding. If not for the sake of sheer justice, then at least for the sake of protecting American interests, President Joe Biden must hold U.S. ally Pakistan accountable to repeal its and actions. While the legislation particularly targets Ahmadi Muslims, it tragically also enables societal discrimination and violence against Pakistan's Christian, Sikh, Hindu, and Shia communities. | | ---|---|--- Debris remains from an Ahmadi mosque that was demolished by an angry mob May, 24, 2018, in Sialkot, Pakistan. Another Ahmadi place of worship was demolished by opponents in Lahore in September 2023. Credit: VOANews And while Pakistan was founded as a secular nation that championed separation of religion and state, pseudo-intellectual clerics gained enough influence over the halls of power to enforce draconian and oppressive legislation targeting the minority Ahmadiyya Muslim Community. Today, Ahmadi Muslims exist in what can only be described as apartheid conditions. As the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom : Ahmadis in Pakistan are subjected to severe legal restrictions and other forms of officially-sanctioned discrimination. Ahmadis are prevented by law from engaging in the full practice of their faith and may face criminal charges for a range of religious practices, including the use of religious terminology. In 1974, the constitution was amended to declare members of the Ahmadi religious community to be "non-Muslims." In 1984, basic acts of Ahmadi worship and interaction were made criminal offenses when sections B and C of Article 298 were added to the penal code. These amendments criminalized Ahmadis "posing" as Muslims, calling their places of worship "mosques," worshipping in non-Ahmadi mosques or public prayer rooms, performing the Muslim call to prayer, using the traditional Islamic greeting in public, or displaying the basic affirmations of the Muslim faith. And as just one example of the absurdity of this persecution, : In April 2023, a 77-year-old lawyer from the Ahmadiyya Community was targeted within the premises of the Sindh High Court by some lawyers on account of his faith. He was accused of hurting the sentiments of Muslims by adding the religiously inclined word 'Syed' as a prefix to his name. However, critics argued that by using the prefix, which is only used by Muslims, the lawyer was trying to pose as a Muslim, and they did not accept that being of the Ahmadiyya faith, he was a Muslim. You read that right. The blasphemy allegation was that his name, Syed, hurt the feelings of orthodox Muslims and therefore he is a criminal. The most prominent and violent attack on Ahmadi Muslims to date occurred on May 28, 2010, when Taliban extremists attacked two Ahmadi mosques in broad daylight, during Friday Jummah Prayer, and . | | ---|---|--- May 29, 2010, Ahmadi Muslims hold a mass funeral for 94 innocent worshippers mass murdered in Pakistan Dozens to hundreds of Ahmadi Muslims are murdered annually with impunity, with hundreds to thousands more in prison, with hundreds of thousands more denied the right to vote, run for office, practice their faith publicly, or even speak freely without the existential consequence of fine, arrest, or even the death penalty. And for those wondering how the authorities might discover that a person belongs to the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community? Ahmadis have special ID cards that forcibly identify their faith, making the targeted persecution an inevitability. The discriminatory laws on the books that ban Ahmadi Muslims from identifying as Muslim, practicing our faith, or even voting, remain on the books. Meanwhile, and by all accounts and testimonies, Ahmadi Muslims are model citizens with a 99% literacy rate, build secular schools and hospitals globally providing free education and healthcare to millions, and have contributed to Pakistan world renowned juggernauts like Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner in . | | ---|---|--- Dr. Abdus Salam was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 for his contribution to the electroweak unification theory But the Government of Pakistan has wiped even his memory from Pakistan's history books, . | | ---|---|--- Nobel Laureate Dr. Abdus Salam's desecrated gravestone in Rabwah, Pakistan Likewise, Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan was a Founding Father of Pakistan who worked closely with Jinnah. Khan was one of the most prolific lawyers and scholars of the 20th century, in contemporary Pakistan he is all but forgotten on account of his devout faith as an Ahmadi Muslim. | | ---|---|--- Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan (Right) served as President of the UN General Assembly In a recent report to the United Nations, the Ahmadiyya Muslim Lawyers Association USA testified to the horrifying escalation that the Government of Pakistan is now engaging in by prosecuting Ahmadi Muslims on terrorism charges for the ' crime' of possessing and selling an : State authorities are also arresting and prosecuting Ahmadi Muslims as "terrorists" under the provisions of Pakistan's primary anti-terrorism legislation, the Anti-Terrorism Act of 1997, for possessing allegedly banned religious publications, arrested and sentenced to five years in prison on blasphemy and terrorism charges for selling copies of the Qur'an and other publications of the community. How ironic that while claiming to stand as the vanguard of Islam, Pakistan criminalizes possession of the Qur 'an itself in ways the West's most ardent Islamophobes could only dream of. The result of this targeted persecution is that despite representing less than 1% of Pakistan's population, "Ahmadi Muslims account for almost 40% of all arrests under Pakistan's anti-blasphemy laws. Over 4,000 Ahmadi Muslims have faced criminal charges for simply practicing their Islamic faith." Perhaps most painful of all during this Eid of Sacrifice, is that while this Eid celebrates and marks the end of the Hajj Pilgrimage--a pilgrimage incumbent upon all able bodied Muslims--Pakistan bans Ahmadi Muslims from completing the Hajj. And still not content with a Hajj ban, . Which brings me back to the vision Jinnah had for Pakistan. Indeed, Pakistan's first Constitution reflected the ideals of religious freedom and minority rights to date denied to Ahmadi Muslims. For example, the Preamble to Pakistan's Constitution declares that Pakistan shall be a state: Wherein adequate provision should be made for the minorities freely to profess and practise [sic] their religions and develop their culture. . .Wherein should be guaranteed fundamental rights including equality of status and of opportunity, equality before law, and freedom of thought, expression, belief, faith, worship and association, and social, economic, and political justice...; Wherein adequate provision shall be made to safeguard the legitimate interests of minorities...; Accordingly, in an address to the United States less than a year after Pakistan's creation, Jinnah to freedom and pluralism to its new ally: In any case Pakistan is not going to be a theocratic State to be ruled by priests with a divine mission. We have many non-Muslims -- Hindus, Christians, and Parsis -- but they are all Pakistanis. They will enjoy the same rights and privileges as any other citizens and will play their rightful part in the affairs of Pakistan. This Eid, as 1.6 billion Muslims celebrate, Ahmadi Muslims in Pakistan will continue to seek that founding promise that has been denied to them for now half a century. While too many in Pakistani leadership and the religious cleric class may not believe in Jinnah's founding promise--Ahmadi Muslims do and always will, and will forever insist on playing our rightful part in the affairs of Pakistan. To support my advocacy for human rights and religious freedom, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.** Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2024 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: Because of my father, I fought back against a life of slavery in China
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lily4Congress Committee
body:
Dad, you are a man of integrity, honor, and dignity.

| [](http://links.eaglecoalition.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=75eef9ac3cde4fb5a622a38c37ea9f84&_e=eCAmy282EhT6NeIlNLvSg9d-LQrG6hApeHx-PgA-parQ0UzdVrKNOKh5OLfuwqd_5N5JHIfJN1GTHPTALOMANKzlSX-dEHScIpw6VrRFU4Dk-5eXNHHpfOYffNZqpyYN6vMql8Mj_N2rEuzz50qeJ-jmtp_ptpgZcVJ4P9TZ-vEZcF_N8IFnrMo4uGAVK0-TroXQ_kE7gsgO7pdXOaAi8EY4sxHNH0X8BQ47LzGnQ4BU4atWfKtDvxFbSmmYJLco6pQFb-4jVugeA8rEUlbpplyaLF1utCqqjRsw8KpENHNxvVooudANbYTXbR_8IWhhUN64Uqnjl3wEgwbr1jpB5pcK4DBl5nPR3xG9pAoBeC-5diqtTTNU_fulAKEuIRISYaACGZJYspCtdHHMvWYtuECd5YdqFd8EBoW5lykUV-4%3D)
---
Fellow American,

I want to tell you a little about my father.

My father was born in 1938 in a village in Sichuan, China.**His parents passed away early in life, and my father became orphaned at the age of six.**

The life of an orphan in China was unforgiving, but thankfully, my father's uncle adopted him. He needed my father to work on the farm; education was expensive, **but he could earn his keep as a farmhand.**_These experiences shaped my father into the man he would become: powerful, steadfast, and honorable—a true fighter._

During Mao's Great Leap Forward campaign, my father was recruited to work for a state-owned steel enterprise in Chongqing. After several years there, he was transferred to Chengdu, where he met my mother and fell in love. They were married after obtaining their respective work unit leaders' _permission_. **I was their first child, one of three, born two years before the terror of the Cultural Revolution ensued.** [](http://links.eaglecoalition.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=75eef9ac3cde4fb5a622a38c37ea9f84&_e=eCAmy282EhT6NeIlNLvSg9d-LQrG6hApeHx-PgA-parQ0UzdVrKNOKh5OLfuwqd_5N5JHIfJN1GTHPTALOMANKzlSX-dEHScIpw6VrRFU4Dk-5eXNHHpfOYffNZqpyYN6vMql8Mj_N2rEuzz50qeJ-jmtp_ptpgZcVJ4P9TZ-vEZcF_N8IFnrMo4uGAVK0-TroXQ_kE7gsgO7pdXOaAi8NKudb6VAmsQxtQoU0YOq7xfGmgzjlnJCVQcPH2pjv2tMT1TZzC3B2sn6QO4COynzzJ-JZh23b5QDDYPzar7wLv8Ri9L7u6xQlcdAhAh_vAsDi__FQJR3cNSqj7srrsqhE4VgBj2AlSvyqymd_qIw-1HRYDHtSt_7Cu5bOdMpgvPYL-3mqUOoZH4YdrYVpdbCA%3D%3D)

My memories began in an employer-provided eight-family row house. Our family of five shared two small rooms with dirt floors and an outdoor kitchen. **There was no plumbing or heating, and we had to share a single water pump and "bathroom" (a hole in the ground) with eight other families.** My parents worked tirelessly, but their positions were low, and our food rations were limited as a result.

**I always remember my father as a hard worker,** waking early each morning to take the bus to work. Public transportation was crowded, and the competition to obtain a seat was so fierce that fights would break out. One day, after nearly being run over in a scuffle to get a seat, my father refused to degrade himself any further and bought a used bike to ride to work.

_Dad's pride denied him submission to the communists' corrupt authoritarianism, and he fought their tyranny at every turn. Honor, however, comes at a price._ They conspired to get rid of him by relocating him to a work site nineteen hours away. He refused to leave my sick mother and his three children despite the commonality of family separations in communist China.

His refusal to relocate earned him a permanent "early retirement" in his late forties. He refused to accept this. And so, he illegally served as a bicycle taxi at night. He had his bike confiscated by the police on multiple occasions and was robbed even more frequently. When seized, he would buy a new bike, and when robbed, he would fight back.

_**My father persevered honorably so that we could survive the brutality of communism. If he had chosen dishonor, perhaps I would not be the person I am today. Because of my father's unwavering moral code, I fought back against a life of slavery in China.**_

After escaping communist China, I brought my parents to the United States, where my father worked in a local Chinese restaurant for a few years. He became a U.S. citizen in 2005 and was later baptized a Christian, something that would have never happened in China. In 2020, at age 82, he was afflicted with COVID and made a full recovery. [](http://links.eaglecoalition.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=75eef9ac3cde4fb5a622a38c37ea9f84&_e=eCAmy282EhT6NeIlNLvSg9d-LQrG6hApeHx-PgA-parQ0UzdVrKNOKh5OLfuwqd_5N5JHIfJN1GTHPTALOMANKzlSX-dEHScIpw6VrRFU4Dk-5eXNHHpfOYffNZqpyYN6vMql8Mj_N2rEuzz50qeJ-jmtp_ptpgZcVJ4P9TZ-vEZcF_N8IFnrMo4uGAVK0-TroXQ_kE7gsgO7pdXOaAi8NKudb6VAmsQxtQoU0YOq7xfGmgzjlnJCVQcPH2pjv2tYeVsZEvEKlGiDdVSIXRe_O2PchM8YnQY0mWUQuBNjiPmR_ialz328ZE7JqlYP0VBJCMx-iLo-OXqXE9v8Fg8lJ3ymPUGjOfbE1YbYaVoa572yma6D_9PMwdWOdRMvNsMTb9o2ebqmQGEcZHTVOuKFQ%3D%3D)

_My father is a man of integrity, honor, and dignity: a small man who cast a large shadow and stood defiantly against the evils of communism. He never asked for much, just the freedom to work and provide for us. He taught me how to live with dignity in defiance of evil. He is my hero._

I am running for Congress to make sure that the evils my family went through in China NEVER make their way into our great nation. **[If you're able to give $1, $2, or even $10 today, I can't tell you what a difference it will make.](http://links.eaglecoalition.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=75eef9ac3cde4fb5a622a38c37ea9f84&_e=eCAmy282EhT6NeIlNLvSg9d-LQrG6hApeHx-PgA-parQ0UzdVrKNOKh5OLfuwqd_5N5JHIfJN1GTHPTALOMANKzlSX-dEHScIpw6VrRFU4Dk-5eXNHHpfOYffNZqpyYN6vMql8Mj_N2rEuzz50qeJ-jmtp_ptpgZcVJ4P9TZ-vEZcF_N8IFnrMo4uGAVK0-TroXQ_kE7gsgO7pdXOaAi8EY4sxHNH0X8BQ47LzGnQ4BU4atWfKtDvxFbSmmYJLcoQkI6gVbXSu04QN_a4vrrpWCtstYTkORWeorO4dd-WiBa37BoazHPSQJ1hc0WWZcjYN8BX8nkqASpIj3UzjfeE2bZ6qwGRjSA2e-hCJt8j0nrIdElQvBjxHS8Ub7-N5vhON7WAu9ZZo82lf5ja8zLe_7crzolTFP5T91JaVjE4nc%3D)**

If you're not, believe me, I understand. **I only ask that you forward this email to 5 of your closest friends.** We must not waver in our fight for Democracy and Freedom, I need you to help me spread the word of what our country faces should we move even further to the Left.

_Thank you so much for reading today.

Lily_

clean_body: Dad, you are a man of integrity, honor, and dignity. | Fellow American, I want to tell you a little about my father. My father was born in 1938 in a village in Sichuan, China.His parents passed away early in life, and my father became orphaned at the age of six. The life of an orphan in China was unforgiving, but thankfully, my father's uncle adopted him. He needed my father to work on the farm; education was expensive, but he could earn his keep as a farmhand.These experiences shaped my father into the man he would become: powerful, steadfast, and honorable—a true fighter. During Mao's Great Leap Forward campaign, my father was recruited to work for a state-owned steel enterprise in Chongqing. After several years there, he was transferred to Chengdu, where he met my mother and fell in love. They were married after obtaining their respective work unit leaders' permission. I was their first child, one of three, born two years before the terror of the Cultural Revolution ensued. My memories began in an employer-provided eight-family row house. Our family of five shared two small rooms with dirt floors and an outdoor kitchen. There was no plumbing or heating, and we had to share a single water pump and "bathroom" (a hole in the ground) with eight other families. My parents worked tirelessly, but their positions were low, and our food rations were limited as a result. I always remember my father as a hard worker, waking early each morning to take the bus to work. Public transportation was crowded, and the competition to obtain a seat was so fierce that fights would break out. One day, after nearly being run over in a scuffle to get a seat, my father refused to degrade himself any further and bought a used bike to ride to work. Dad's pride denied him submission to the communists' corrupt authoritarianism, and he fought their tyranny at every turn. Honor, however, comes at a price. They conspired to get rid of him by relocating him to a work site nineteen hours away. He refused to leave my sick mother and his three children despite the commonality of family separations in communist China. His refusal to relocate earned him a permanent "early retirement" in his late forties. He refused to accept this. And so, he illegally served as a bicycle taxi at night. He had his bike confiscated by the police on multiple occasions and was robbed even more frequently. When seized, he would buy a new bike, and when robbed, he would fight back. My father persevered honorably so that we could survive the brutality of communism. If he had chosen dishonor, perhaps I would not be the person I am today. Because of my father's unwavering moral code, I fought back against a life of slavery in China. After escaping communist China, I brought my parents to the United States, where my father worked in a local Chinese restaurant for a few years. He became a U.S. citizen in 2005 and was later baptized a Christian, something that would have never happened in China. In 2020, at age 82, he was afflicted with COVID and made a full recovery. My father is a man of integrity, honor, and dignity: a small man who cast a large shadow and stood defiantly against the evils of communism. He never asked for much, just the freedom to work and provide for us. He taught me how to live with dignity in defiance of evil. He is my hero. I am running for Congress to make sure that the evils my family went through in China NEVER make their way into our great nation. If you're not, believe me, I understand. I only ask that you forward this email to 5 of your closest friends. We must not waver in our fight for Democracy and Freedom, I need you to help me spread the word of what our country faces should we move even further to the Left. Thank you so much for reading today. Lily | Paid for by Lily4Congress Committee Lily4Congress Committee PO Box 406 Weare, NH 03281 Copyright (C) 2024 Lily4Congress, All rights reserved.

name: Josh Harder
subject: Hi Peter,
body:
Just wanted to say hello.
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta0abf7dd-4f49-49d3-a4ab-bf8f6fe3c00c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| | | Hi Peter, My name is Josh Harder - I’m a husband, a new dad, and the Democratic Congressman from California’s 9th district, one of the most competitive races in the country. ☝️That’s me with my wife Pam and our daughter Lillian - we also recently welcomed our baby girl Karina to the family! I want to reach out to introduce myself today and tell you a bit about why my race in California is a must win if we’re going to take back the House from MAGA extremists and hold the line against Donald Trump. [If you can split a donation between me and Blue Amp Action to help us win this competitive 2024 race, I’ll leave a link here.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb6a0f658-f36d-42f9-b886-b1596ed36d5e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Here’s why you should care:
1. I represent a seat in California’s Central Valley which has been named one of the most competitive in the country. Democrats recently added me to their Frontline list of most vulnerable members because we desperately need more resources to keep this seat blue.

2. I’m one of Donald Trump and Mike Johnson’s top targets this year. They’ve announced $2 million in TV reservations in our media market, and GOP SuperPACs are already spreading disinformation to our latino communities.

3. My opponent this year has been called the GOP’s “Dream Candidate” to run against me. He’s the Mayor of Stockton, the biggest city in the district, and he’s on the record saying he’ll vote against women’s reproductive rights.

4. A terrifying new poll shows us down by 4 points. If that’s the case, there is no way Democrats will take back the Speaker’s gavel. We have to make sure these aren’t the results we see on Election Day.

5. The end-of-quarter deadline is coming up and we need your help to have a strong showing. We can’t afford to be outraised by our Republican opponent, who is getting a huge boost from Trump’s guilty verdict.

[That’s why I’m reaching out today to ask for your support. I don’t take corporate PAC money, so I really need your help. Will you please split a donation between me and Blue Amp Action so we have the resources to win CA-9? ](https://default.salsalabs.org/T74dedd47-079b-4ec0-9745-bfcdf5085eae/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | | [DONATE](https://default.salsalabs.org/T40e6d58d-af7c-4005-be21-98e4f8e3dd67/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| \- Josh

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T617e0f5f-d100-44a1-8109-d9b50928c7ae/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Sign on to pass the Transgender Bill of Rights in Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
With an unprecedented onslaught of anti-trans attacks, we need urgent action. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWzUOzUNdQEA5K3octjBN0Ynjj8E-M0aHspmj0NqscayDEP9Cx6F2Ptdr5X3OpCtQvfrMWqw-60RwyPGStxqukzq7NdkquvGh-F7LSo0p8HgoTdOP4zkTMHOaPm_75cnrhfp3iudKw5aGZBxyaJQ4F-iwslz7Ego6p-9m-r7hM0tUkOyxfdcODFJLKhGYMinCE9lqqIum3ZMW7dE_b5wBRm3scRTaJPFk7zl6AqVFQMRU/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h0/h001.CW6tdJIXH6qNOAzz1qzrzQ3frJuzPIeRK7Roq_YRHCQ)
---
Friend, Republicans at the state and federal level are attacking transgender and nonbinary people. We need to counter these attacks and strengthen protections to ensure that all people can live safely and with dignity. I’m a proud co-sponsor of the _Transgender Bill of Rights_ , a Congressional resolution calling on the federal government to protect the rights of transgender and nonbinary people, including ensuring that trans people have access to gender-affirming care. [**Please add your name to tell Congress: Pass the**** _Trans Bill of Rights_****this Pride month! People’s lives are at stake.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39qcB9DwacTEXzEHNqM-TpHX6e0pywfjKe55pttrrgjUb4wEVW6Rn7QFLE-sMGOfn3ZZJehpRXYzxxBjMW3x8PL4jOUgH9WzgyOIx57Pb3bBp0e4S9G0zLY77WMYIrMlCORvlzUkqwzArqD-F4nvmvUC_nU4o-Et2rmvqHVwuqZtOCbMAcOdPjYsDVIT1znwj-ogKPTZQYjJqV8M37uXLy9DRK_xFzESstFc_y9W6JJDt-Ft_exRTkTQCEgoGXREE_KeJzgSmMpProbGKYWuop6yxwntqMiiA6eDhSQmhDU6sQlebhU5LuRUP02TeX0NrqZolJgynfIinnqkHpab9_fonh7UqGCzZRHPXBzZOseOkUqsdcl-mlNQf-tyW0UytYiF3PDqCsgT6a_zGiPDFBt1SHWfsgkkMMWEQuCAsOJfQaGI-u8gAzqACGwKtJIqSd6agr0jXrlF0JubS4QrbAs/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h1/h001.Zs8PLtIb_SikDn9aN6aD4PwAMFZaAXdAJHga2U0t9fY) In 2023, state legislators introduced 500 anti-LGBTQ+ laws, including bans on gender-affirming care. Some states and municipalities have also passed “forced outing” policies, mandating school and government employees out trans people to their parents. This can be extremely dangerous, risking physical violence and homelessness for trans youth. Last year, the Republican majority in the U.S. House of Representatives passed a bill to ban trans athletes from women’s and girls’ sports. President Biden has said he’ll veto such a bill if it passes the Senate and comes to his desk. However, if Trump or another Republican were President and the Senate had a Republican majority, bills like this would become law. We cannot leave people’s rights up to the unelected right-wing Supreme Court or a future Republican presidential administration. Instead, **we must enshrine these fundamental rights into federal law as soon as possible.** By passing the _Trans Bill of Rights_ , Congress would affirm “that it is the duty of the Federal Government to develop and implement” legislation that would protect transgender and nonbinary Americans’ access to healthcare, employment, housing, credit, public accommodations and services, inclusive education, and more. The resolution urges investments in mental health services designed for transgender and nonbinary people, as well as community services to prevent anti-trans violence. This is essential, because more than 40% of trans Americans have attempted suicide, and they’re at least 4 times more likely than their cisgender peers to be victims of violent crime. The _Trans Bill of Rights_ also calls for banning harmful “conversion therapy” practices and for designating an office within the Department of Justice’s Civil Rights Division to enforce the civil rights of transgender people. [**Please sign on now to urge Congress to pass the**** _Trans Bill of Rights_****. With an unprecedented onslaught of anti-trans attacks, we need urgent action.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39qcB9DwacTEXzEHNqM-TpHX6e0pywfjKe55pttrrgjUb4wEVW6Rn7QFLE-sMGOfn3ZZJehpRXYzxxBjMW3x8PL4jOUgH9WzgyOIx57Pb3bBp0e4S9G0zLY77WMYIrMlCORvlzUkqwzArqD-F4nvmvUC_nU4o-Et2rmvqHVwuqZtOCbMAcOdPjYsDVIT1znwj-ogKPTZQYjJqV8M37uXLy9s8oAzTYD-22S_1CVmPdknWX-FHZQ3QSbtMB5RSdDab80xIyX9ce2tX1HFCnfCDE_RribF5YZ6M_-wQxszlGf-QGD0OTCdi4hnC5xFpR-RU8rFC-OOmpEmdHRBghqi04BgDvkSYZfL_7Kt7Iad-t-8MBcHiFFYxUKuCgkExzGW75-2UuvpuT56dlag8nXNfO2bidxWp5zzY0uBJ6ZPFOr9Q9ZHyXDhJCkLhlc7lY-AAS-kS8C0260uRt6OwMjCIuX/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h2/h001.GiyKn93ELHcc3syDK80ROgpW0U-1yE_E8nQoCK7YYEw) | [SIGN ON](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39qcB9DwacTEXzEHNqM-TpHX6e0pywfjKe55pttrrgjUb4wEVW6Rn7QFLE-sMGOfn3ZZJehpRXYzxxBjMW3x8PL4jOUgH9WzgyOIx57Pb3bBp0e4S9G0zLY77WMYIrMlCORvlzUkqwzArqD-F4nvmvUC_nU4o-Et2rmvqHVwuqZtOCbMAcOdPjYsDVIT1znwj-ogKPTZQYjJqV8M37uXLy9G4yGETdRsktFWgUePKjSkfCVAeLhMLMe-uPWYAMFX3GXTxSNMFJ2S6nWM5H2h5dTuzuEaVaCq_h6_A7VhxtHiu-o2NlvxZjUxAEVLeC_cOdExVgTkrpy4t1tYwIK2BVBkx2IoMm7oQKlrk7Mo-PL6AkzBaG0XA1-DO9UsavYQ1a9C7PipAO1Y1Yd24WXvB3bZipW1znUFe3NQtBpKcFUnHDnq51_JG5gAhG4KWrpOPDNAmTHfgUzs2ovWyRUM0Kx/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h3/h001.rNsEC2i-vMjAUr_7DsveKImarwLs5Ewe1SqWl5CVpGg)
---
As Rep. Pramila Jayapal, who introduced this bill in the House of Representatives, said:
> _“Our Trans Bill of Rights says clearly to the trans community across the country that we see you and we will stand with you to ensure you are protected and given the dignity and respect that every person should have.”_
No matter your gender identity or sexual orientation, I will keep fighting in solidarity for your safety, liberation, and human rights.
Always serving you,
Rashida
P.S. If you’re a trans or non-binary adult in crisis, the Trans Lifeline’s Hotline is a peer support line at 877-565-8860. For young people under 25 years old, the Trevor Project offers support if you call 866-488-7386, text START to 678678, or by [online chat](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYbPnuI7wEsqvik-aGbg6TVHoU2QsjfIzm8-7oHMsCxZ-UgrfSSGxIF5zBuFur2cKTko1-rOa4JBDXKeb7eQzI90BqN2dGaXDsprqFTVsJE8KOJxe8-vdCIgRLxzA5JvYM7elrH4kAqdzgNh6KmxWdywub5KNujxq_UqDv9pwcdmqadRKuSNCyzN0DrnQ9CH5sSM6rDyw-wIbhYSAHJ8m_0R36lJ50PRgse_NFsu9U1Lw8bKwooCwLRWTWQILLMCL5kjlBXfJp16Cfi1dpKzgI5URq2aZamH_hU4yjvVb3owSOwKCW_B9x-WqaLLCN2V2stpjaJzp9iYoHjWFPHUgp4iuhThkRFUEkDHYTGmM7bl/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h4/h001.wq_BthLZdHRVct0dBbmGuEbt47Gu2yjBCh-YopDNWTk).
If you want to do more to support your LGBTQ+ neighbors, here’s [a resource](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYbPnuI7wEsqvik-aGbg6TXS5XleNFSUYPhfKAbYMjdj2kqCVAmZ0PsU2uID4Cg7lvEprzHifa-bsRAkxR6F8mMoY0Q0XKRQ5x6UIUjQP8XCWo-45OaG-fgSrr65VxBanSWCR2O2x956HCc4QSEk62eStxVly4M1GIG9dUUPN_sW-IpzEmfZm-GNlYLVZL1VNTm1htmInJU_HiSNfBmiskwdPZLjtyPEEpmpk-a4-phLOWpEzz67aXICCu34kBxs7Ptay2xIBVI61ZeZblY4tnaLpKEuZpOz52MyPocmNru6PCDGLibwsAl-lCu5oJiuxbWIybky0vKLmtoSPyKx-krzXO6fAT4p_9-GnvFYYb1oRgpAtv3S7FLbYHPw-y7DdZG1Q_f83glrHNwtdsbYp58TRSE7Srpevvs1okO9LaiEWe6xcQIE259MhRHgWwSemA/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h5/h001.XF7r6hcXL2A-F68_VVakhZIogcvIP9VezIV-BdaYStM) from the Trevor Project about being an ally to trans and non-binary youth, and [a guide](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYbPnuI7wEsqvik-aGbg6TXS5XleNFSUYPhfKAbYMjdjJEjHCDm_7dsrkaDt19rt_tin2xDsUuGmuJtCDozpgr4H3zhtfj8EFQpWSHFlg8DEW19PQ0Vqd_wAek6e_z7O9eI8F1yem6LFCO-rkfg6tX4pFpQ9fSj_FcLhqGfJk_4Tmjb1RH1xIr46LP3QxjReySUgvMToYRZu9ipGHp3L0Y0zVcRV5luLLZGMs5MkrZsQIILdn1BoEM-XmPhCMLTEfVmXZ2fK2zx6gThMR8Tty_LY6X2mon0o3n-dGgUY6WFR6r97vtjoqisWqr-4zUk7xlNvpJkj8v4u1rPumjIx3st2mWSAnFV1YsErYgsYUe-BBp1ALNNsxjORBLVcwz6dTVqKq2y9O-whyphMgK6TE421xsGgGO9p6KeyaHIENye1Du6SwkDPVj2RFbfhMwsFZw/479/zrvBhKkeTRK4xkPlXJfnyA/h6/h001.wgXSDN0N2JUgmukMJHOM1Gl311gzJztbyyqUqTsKs_M) for creating safer spaces in schools for LGBTQ+ people.

clean_body: With an unprecedented onslaught of anti-trans attacks, we need urgent action. | | | Friend, Republicans at the state and federal level are attacking transgender and nonbinary people. We need to counter these attacks and strengthen protections to ensure that all people can live safely and with dignity. I’m a proud co-sponsor of the Transgender Bill of Rights , a Congressional resolution calling on the federal government to protect the rights of transgender and nonbinary people, including ensuring that trans people have access to gender-affirming care. In 2023, state legislators introduced 500 anti-LGBTQ+ laws, including bans on gender-affirming care. Some states and municipalities have also passed “forced outing” policies, mandating school and government employees out trans people to their parents. This can be extremely dangerous, risking physical violence and homelessness for trans youth. Last year, the Republican majority in the U.S. House of Representatives passed a bill to ban trans athletes from women’s and girls’ sports. President Biden has said he’ll veto such a bill if it passes the Senate and comes to his desk. However, if Trump or another Republican were President and the Senate had a Republican majority, bills like this would become law. We cannot leave people’s rights up to the unelected right-wing Supreme Court or a future Republican presidential administration. Instead, we must enshrine these fundamental rights into federal law as soon as possible. By passing the Trans Bill of Rights , Congress would affirm “that it is the duty of the Federal Government to develop and implement” legislation that would protect transgender and nonbinary Americans’ access to healthcare, employment, housing, credit, public accommodations and services, inclusive education, and more. The resolution urges investments in mental health services designed for transgender and nonbinary people, as well as community services to prevent anti-trans violence. This is essential, because more than 40% of trans Americans have attempted suicide, and they’re at least 4 times more likely than their cisgender peers to be victims of violent crime. The Trans Bill of Rights also calls for banning harmful “conversion therapy” practices and for designating an office within the Department of Justice’s Civil Rights Division to enforce the civil rights of transgender people. | As Rep. Pramila Jayapal, who introduced this bill in the House of Representatives, said: “Our Trans Bill of Rights says clearly to the trans community across the country that we see you and we will stand with you to ensure you are protected and given the dignity and respect that every person should have.” No matter your gender identity or sexual orientation, I will keep fighting in solidarity for your safety, liberation, and human rights. Always serving you, Rashida P.S. If you’re a trans or non-binary adult in crisis, the Trans Lifeline’s Hotline is a peer support line at 877-565-8860. For young people under 25 years old, the Trevor Project offers support if you call 866-488-7386, text START to 678678, or by . If you want to do more to support your LGBTQ+ neighbors, here’s from the Trevor Project about being an ally to trans and non-binary youth, and for creating safer spaces in schools for LGBTQ+ people. Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Father’s Day this year
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#] I have often said that being a dad is the most rewarding job I have. Learning from my two children, Ellie and Ben, and watching them grow has been a remarkable experience. They remind me every day how lucky I am. I have a loving family, an amazing partner in Marla, and a job where I can make change happen.

While I’m thinking about the present today, I’m also looking back to where I came from. My mom was a teacher and my dad started his own business. Working for my dad when I was young is when I learned my most important lesson, “work hard and give back.”

I’ve lived by these words my entire life, and are the main reason why I’m in Congress. I get to give back by solving our problems for and as a community. Our District is a family because we lift each other up and look at people as people — not a mindset. We must keep fighting to create a safer and more equitable world for our kids and the next generation.

I am so grateful for your support. Together we can succeed in securing the future all our kids deserve.

Wishing you and yours a very happy Father’s Day.

Yours,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. clean_body: [#] I have often said that being a dad is the most rewarding job I have. Learning from my two children, Ellie and Ben, and watching them grow has been a remarkable experience. They remind me every day how lucky I am. I have a loving family, an amazing partner in Marla, and a job where I can make change happen. While I’m thinking about the present today, I’m also looking back to where I came from. My mom was a teacher and my dad started his own business. Working for my dad when I was young is when I learned my most important lesson, “work hard and give back.” I’ve lived by these words my entire life, and are the main reason why I’m in Congress. I get to give back by solving our problems for and as a community. Our District is a family because we lift each other up and look at people as people — not a mindset. We must keep fighting to create a safer and more equitable world for our kids and the next generation. I am so grateful for your support. Together we can succeed in securing the future all our kids deserve. Wishing you and yours a very happy Father’s Day. Yours, Josh Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. If you'd like to support his campaign click here [] , if you'd like to unsubscribe click here. [] Thank you for being on Team Gottheimer. body:
Happy Father’s Day!

Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/KuMB1syMs0W9O3v5MuCvqQ2?unsubscribedata=QqKguFWVyDNpELZXI%2F8qSJlUsJ0X0q5RUJjmXXjW9Uzmz72mPWAxe3nIqRywEL7X5WtMm1u%2FT%2FrQ9000uyc0YHdXk%2FzoDFRgaA7LLPugRmapUrSi01AsU%2BNpal0Aid0Q6vuuRbHkhfscYaOhJAXSyB8p7E0hh0%2B%2BASSi089H049%2F38Ld2xHnDllm4TsFa9TMp5B8x4wXInyQl6VGwKCWWCAdX2g%2Bw6HMHsD9zlR%2FgaA%2BqO%2BhM0tzdIP2R0XRqA%2BAPexB4DRoIy09I3g3aVU2Gy81KhHXIh%2Bhx8Q5AdYSB3BWewy0UtZHQJw9x6WXUqoYNShZH9obaovpu%2BSeVFncYjxFFbrYWEwZy9QhQWI2%2BsgMLOU7JR%2FXGJLhx3KRppmZnzWt%2FNCn%2FWUdh0T9eavBUSNIBoOfKMU8cb88pSaHEyQaxKfy1hWkKxkMVtH%2B9fmz)
---
| | Hey Peter, This Father’s Day, I want to take a moment to reflect on what family means to me. Not only is my family the cornerstone of my mission to get our Montana back, but my family is the most important thing in my life. Being Lander and Badge’s father is my greatest achievement — and there is no greater privilege than being able to raise them in Montana alongside my incredible wife Sara. Sara’s guidance, strength, and kindness are an unwavering reminder of what it means to be a successful parent — thanks to her, I am able to thrive as a father. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/87016816/479756781/-1483394401?source=SBD_RB_EM_FR_061624_FathersDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDgzNDciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDM3NTUyZDItZmQyYi1lZjExLTg2ZDItNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q5MQCdBRd9e5UYo0RuC2pRXUleVHMxNfxaQIwUefM94=) I am so proud of the strong, well-mannered, and civically engaged young men my sons have become. They have been outstanding advocates for our Montana constitutional right to a clean and healthful environment. Their commitment to progress is a testament to their Montana upbringing. And like a lot of Montana kids, they can throw a tight flyfishing loop, pack out an elk quarter, and row the rapids because it’s part of who they are. I am worried about whether or not our boys will be able to afford to live here. Each member of the Busse family is committed to uplifting every single Montanan and championing our Montana way of life. We are going to fight for this place and for kids like Lander and Badge who want to make a life here. Peter, I’m in this race to bring our core family values of our deep love of Montana and our communities to the governor’s seat. My mission is to ensure my sons and the next generation of Montanans have the resources they need to not only continue to call Montana home, but to thrive in their communities. [Will you please consider donating $10 or anything you can to support my campaign and help me celebrate Father’s Day?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/87016817/479756782/-1483394401?amount=10&source=SBD_RB_EM_FR_061624_FathersDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDgzNDciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDM3NTUyZDItZmQyYi1lZjExLTg2ZDItNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q5MQCdBRd9e5UYo0RuC2pRXUleVHMxNfxaQIwUefM94=) | | [DONATE NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/87016821/479756783/-1483394401?amount=10&source=SBD_RB_EM_FR_061624_FathersDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDgzNDciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDM3NTUyZDItZmQyYi1lZjExLTg2ZDItNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q5MQCdBRd9e5UYo0RuC2pRXUleVHMxNfxaQIwUefM94=)
---
To all the dads out there, thank you for all you do. Happy Father’s Day!
Thanks,
Ryan

clean_body: Happy Father’s Day! Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Hey Peter, This Father’s Day, I want to take a moment to reflect on what family means to me. Not only is my family the cornerstone of my mission to get our Montana back, but my family is the most important thing in my life. Being Lander and Badge’s father is my greatest achievement — and there is no greater privilege than being able to raise them in Montana alongside my incredible wife Sara. Sara’s guidance, strength, and kindness are an unwavering reminder of what it means to be a successful parent — thanks to her, I am able to thrive as a father. = I am so proud of the strong, well-mannered, and civically engaged young men my sons have become. They have been outstanding advocates for our Montana constitutional right to a clean and healthful environment. Their commitment to progress is a testament to their Montana upbringing. And like a lot of Montana kids, they can throw a tight flyfishing loop, pack out an elk quarter, and row the rapids because it’s part of who they are. I am worried about whether or not our boys will be able to afford to live here. Each member of the Busse family is committed to uplifting every single Montanan and championing our Montana way of life. We are going to fight for this place and for kids like Lander and Badge who want to make a life here. Peter, I’m in this race to bring our core family values of our deep love of Montana and our communities to the governor’s seat. My mission is to ensure my sons and the next generation of Montanans have the resources they need to not only continue to call Montana home, but to thrive in their communities. = | | = To all the dads out there, thank you for all you do. Happy Father’s Day! Thanks, Ryan | | | ---|---|--- = For more than 25 years, Ryan was a successful gun industry executive. But he also saw how unchecked extremism could take away American freedoms and even jeopardize our democracy. Ryan sees the same thing happening to Montana: wealthy extremist Greg Gianforte bought his seat with his own millions, and now he’s raising our taxes in order to give tax breaks to corporations, making Montana unaffordable, defunding our public schools, locking up our public lands, hijacking our freedoms, and rigging the system for the rich. Ryan chose Raph Graybill as his running mate because no Montanan has done more to protect constitutional rights and freedoms from Greg Gianforte. Since Gianforte became governor, Raph has blocked more than a dozen different unconstitutional laws that invaded Montanans’ constitutional rights. Ryan and Raph are running as champions for ALL Montanans and to Get Our Montana Back!** | = = PAID FOR BY BUSSE FOR MONTANA • DEMOCRAT • PO BOX 8537 • KALISPELL, MT 59904 If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from us, . name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: “That’s my Betsy."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Warren for Senate: https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/370?source=em-header&t=1001&akid=2221%2E4032634%2EHRx3yO

It’s Father’s Day, and if this holiday is hitting you hard,
my heart goes out to you.

I’m wishing I could give my daddy a call.

After my mother died, I called him every night. Most weekends, we watched
sports together, with him in Oklahoma and me in Massachusetts. We called
back and forth on the phone after a really good (or bad) play.

I wanted to keep him company, and I wanted to hear his voice.

He taught me to dream big. In the 1920s, he had big dreams of his own: He
wanted to fly airplanes. He was barely out of high school when he rebuilt
a two-passenger, open-cockpit airplane and taught himself to fly above the
prairies of eastern Oklahoma. I always pictured him landing and taking off
in vast wheat fields, a tiny plane in an immense blue sky.

He survived the double blows of the Dust Bowl and the Great Depression in
the small town where he grew up. Decades later, both my parents still
talked of bank failures and families who lost their farms during some of
the toughest times for our country.

By the time World War II came along, they already had three young boys.
Daddy tried to enlist to be a fighter pilot in the war, but the Army Air
Forces (as it was known then) said he was too old, or at least that’s the
explanation I heard.

When the war finally ended, he desperately wanted a job flying the new
passenger planes for one of the fast-growing airlines like TWA or
American. But that didn’t work out either. My mother told me that those
jobs also went to younger men.

So he moved from one job to another: He was a salesman, he fixed cars, he
ended up as a janitor. He liked working with his hands, doing repairs
around the house. And like a zillion other families, we got by.

When I was a senior in high school, I started thinking about college. I
wanted to be a teacher, and that meant I needed a college diploma. But my
mother said it was out of the question. She pointed out that we couldn’t
afford college, that she and Daddy just didn’t make enough money. Besides,
he’d had a heart attack, and now it took both of my parents’ paychecks to
manage.

I knew how much my parents sacrificed for my future and for my brothers’
futures, but I wanted to teach. It was my big dream, and I wouldn’t give
up.

My mother kept saying no, and we argued back and forth. Then Daddy
surprised both of us, saying: “Let her try, Polly.” And I was off and
running.

There were lots of bumps and wrong turns along the way. I got married at
19 (it didn’t work out). I moved. I dropped out of school. But eventually,
I made it.

Whatever I did, my daddy believed in me and my big dreams. He would say,
“That’s my Betsy."

In truth, I think he found it pretty miraculous that his baby girl had
ended up a teacher. He nearly busted his buttons the first time I was
quoted in the newspaper.

When he got sick, I came across a video series about the airplanes of
World War II. I bought all the videotapes and took them to him. I was sure
he would love seeing the old planes, that he would point out this or that
plane he remembered from the war. But the only thing he wanted to talk
about was how much he missed my mother.

For months — for years, actually — after he died, I would see or hear
something and think, Oh, I’ll tell Daddy about that. And then I’d get a
little jolt all over again. He was gone.

I would not be here if he hadn’t pushed open a door so that I could have a
chance. That is how this daughter of a janitor ended up as a public school
teacher, a law professor, and a United States senator. And our story isn’t
unique. Parents all across our country are working hard to give their
children the opportunities they themselves never had.

That kind of sacrifice — a sacrifice fueled by love — is why I’m in this
fight all the way. All of our children deserve a chance to succeed. And
all of our parents deserve the peace of mind of knowing their hard work
created a better future for their children and grandchildren. That is the
promise of America, and I’m fighting to make sure we keep that promise.

My daddy is gone, but I want to wish him a Happy Father’s Day anyway — and
to all the fathers, and father figures, who are trying their hardest to
give their children a strong future, setting the example every day of what
it means to care for the people you love.

Elizabeth

| Fellow Republicans and conservatives – Happy Father’s Day! It’s the final stretch, and our campaign is feeling the momentum! An update from the campaign trail:
* Don't forget! Your ballot must be _received_ by your county clerk on Tuesday, June 25th. Wondering where to return your ballot? Use this link: [https://www.sos.state.co.us/pubs/elections/VIP.html](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNTE3MzI1NjgsXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiJkMTk3ZTcxNzQ5ZGYxYTRhIn0)
* My far-left opponent, Yadira Caraveo, continues to side with Joe Biden and liberal activists. This week, she actually [voted against](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNTQ4NzgyOTksXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiI2MzI2MjYzNGExZDRmNzUwIn0) funding for our troops because she wanted the government to fund abortions and maintain DEI offices at the Department of Defense. She voted against a much-needed nearly 20% increase in pay for junior enlisted servicemen and women.
* Donald Trump has endorsed our campaign, joining dozens of other Colorado and national leaders, including House Speaker Mike Johnson, U.S. Senator Tom Cotton, 11 of the district’s 22 mayors, the Colorado Fraternal Order of Police, and dozens of other local officials. Check out the complete list on our [website](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNTgwMjQwMjksXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiJiZWE2NjRhMDBiZDIxMTRjIn0).
* Please help us keep our ads on the air and support our tremendous volunteer program with a contribution -- $25, $50, $100, anything up to the $6,600 allowed under federal law. Your support goes straight to the campaign, to help support our television, radio, and digital ads and even feed our volunteers.
* Be sure to follow us on [Facebook](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNjExNjk3NTksXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiI3YzMyYzcxNjg2NzExMWFjIn0) and [X](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNjQzMTU0ODksXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiJmYzEzNTNjOGViNWQyZWE2In0)!

The last 9 months, since we launched this campaign, have been a tremendous journey for me and my family. With just 9 days until the primary, I want to thank each and every one of you who has contributed time or resources to get us to this point. Your generosity means more than you can imagine. In Freedom, Gabe Evans P.S. As a former cop, I know how destructive the far-left, “defund the police” agenda has been to public safety. Help me get to Washington and close the border, end the lawlessness, and back the blue. Your [contribution](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/tf/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMjQzMTU0MjEyNjc0NjEyMTgsXCJyXCI6MTI0MzE1NDIyODYzMzkzOTc4fSIsInMiOiI4YzQwZWQ2NmJlODQ3ZTFiIn0) of $25, $50, $100 – anything up to the maximum $6,600 allowed by law – will help my “fight back” movement and bring conservative change to Washington, D.C.
